message:
feat(render-term): add terminal minimap and diff rendering (+818 lines)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -105,14 +105,14 @@
 {"id":"bd-2hq","title":"Build adversarial security test corpus for parser and renderers","description":"Create an adversarial test corpus and automated validation pipeline to harden FrankenMermaid against malicious or pathological inputs while preserving best-effort rendering behavior.

Coverage areas:
1. SVG/HTML injection attempts in labels, links, classDef, markdown, and directives (including script tags, event handlers, data URLs, foreignObject, malformed entities).
2. Parser stress inputs: deeply nested blocks, huge fanout graphs, unicode edge cases, malformed UTF-8 surrogates, repeated delimiters, and intentionally ambiguous syntax to stress recovery logic.
3. Resource abuse patterns: extremely large identifiers/labels, high-edge-density graphs, degenerate cycles, and repeated fragments that can trigger quadratic behavior.
4. DOT bridge abuse cases: attribute injection, escaped-string edge cases, malformed subgraphs.
5. WASM/browser safety checks: ensure no runtime panics and no unsafe DOM/script injection vectors in produced output strings.

Implementation requirements:
- Add fixtures under `tests/security_corpus/` with labeled expected outcomes (`allow-with-warning`, `sanitize`, `reject-fragment`, etc. where applicable).
- Add dedicated security e2e script mode that emits detailed JSON + human logs including input id, sanitizer actions, diagnostics, render timing, and final verdict.
- Integrate into CI as blocking checks with deterministic fixture ordering and reproducible output.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Security corpus includes at least 150 adversarial fixtures spanning parser, layout, SVG, terminal, and WASM paths.
- Running security checks never panics and produces deterministic verdicts/logs across repeated runs.
- Sanitization and diagnostic behavior is explicitly asserted in automated tests; regressions fail CI.
- Security e2e logging includes fixture id, threat class, sanitizer/normalizer decisions, timing, and pass/fail reason.
- CI blocks on security suite failure and publishes machine-readable report artifacts.
- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.
- Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic.
- Comprehensive automated tests are added/updated for this issue: focused unit tests + relevant integration coverage for touched behavior and edge cases.
- End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons).
- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.
- Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable).
- Quality gates pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and relevant `cargo test` scope.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T18:42:44.396639378Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:02:57.058477158Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","security","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hq","depends_on_id":"bd-2xl.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2hq","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2hy","title":"Implement pie and donut chart rendering","description":"Implement pie/donut chart rendering in SVG. Uses polar coordinates (not graph layout). Features: (1) SVG arc paths for slices computed from percentage values. (2) Color assignment from theme palette. (3) Labels: value and percentage on each slice (inside if large enough, outside with leader line if small). (4) Legend with color swatches. (5) Title text above chart. (6) Donut variant: inner radius creates a hole. (7) Exploded slice option for emphasis. (8) Animation: slices draw clockwise on load. Pure geometry computation -- no layout engine involvement.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All functionality explicitly described above is implemented fully; no scope reduction, no feature deletions, and no silent behavioral regressions.
- Code is modular, documented where needed, and integrated with existing crates/interfaces without compatibility shims or duplicated logic.
- Comprehensive automated tests are added/updated for this issue: focused unit tests + relevant integration coverage for touched behavior and edge cases.
- End-to-end validation is covered where applicable (CLI and/or WASM path) with detailed structured logging (inputs, detection results, node/edge counts, output sizes, timings, and pass/fail reasons).
- Diagnostics are actionable: failures/warnings include precise context and suggested remediation.
- Determinism and stability expectations are verified for affected outputs (layout/render/serialization as applicable).
- Quality gates pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo check --all-targets`, and relevant `cargo test` scope.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:38:12.515204779Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T01:41:59.490793596Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["chart","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hy","depends_on_id":"bd-1y5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:37:48Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw","title":"EPIC: Feature Parity with FrankenTUI Reference Implementation","description":"# Feature Parity EPIC (Merged Program)

Purpose:
- Achieve feature parity with the FrankenTUI reference implementation while reusing shared reliability infrastructure from bd-3uz.
- Prevent duplicate implementation tracks by making bd-2nw parity focused and bd-3uz infrastructure focused.

Ownership split:
- bd-3uz owns cross cutting correctness, determinism, degradation, diagnostics, and release gate machinery.
- bd-2nw owns extraction parity, reference behavior comparison, and parity evidence production.

Reference sources:
- /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid.rs
- /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_layout.rs
- /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_render.rs
- /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_diff.rs
- /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_minimap.rs
- /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/diagram_layout.rs
- /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/diagram.rs
- /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/dot_parser.rs
- /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/canvas.rs

Mandatory verification policy for this EPIC:
- Child beads must produce comprehensive unit, integration, and E2E evidence with detailed structured logs.
- Parity evidence must be machine readable and must be consumed by release gate decisions.
- Any unresolved parity gap must be explicitly classified and linked to follow up beads.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:50.885242727Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:27:12.857279767Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","extraction","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:20:00.060677307Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":63,"issue_id":"bd-2nw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation Strategy

This EPIC follows a bottom-up approach:

1. **Foundation** (bd-2nw.3, bd-2nw.4): Complete core algorithms
2. **Extraction** (bd-2nw.1, bd-2nw.2, bd-2nw.5, bd-2nw.11): Extract FrankenTUI features
3. **Enhancement** (bd-2nw.10, bd-2nw.13, bd-2nw.14): Add missing capabilities
4. **Quality** (bd-2nw.6, bd-2nw.7, bd-2nw.8, bd-2nw.12): Harden and optimize
5. **Validation** (bd-2nw.9, bd-2nw.15): Prove parity and correctness

## Parallel Work Streams

Agents can work in parallel on:
- **Stream A**: Parser completion (bd-2nw.10, bd-2nw.13)
- **Stream B**: Layout enhancement (bd-2nw.3, bd-2nw.14)
- **Stream C**: Render extraction (bd-2nw.1, bd-2nw.2, bd-2nw.5, bd-2nw.11)
- **Stream D**: Quality/testing (bd-2nw.7, bd-2nw.9)

## Key Metrics

Track these throughout implementation:
- Lines extracted vs reference total
- Test coverage percentage
- Performance vs reference
- Diagram types fully supported","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:20:37Z"},{"id":64,"issue_id":"bd-2nw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Merge update:  now explicitly layers parity/extraction goals on top of shared runtime correctness infrastructure in . This prevents duplicate implementation tracks and makes parity evidence release-critical.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:53Z"},{"id":65,"issue_id":"bd-2nw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Merge update: bd-2nw tasks now explicitly layer parity extraction and reference comparison on top of shared bd-3uz runtime guarantees. This avoids duplicate implementation tracks and makes parity evidence release critical.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:27:12Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2nw.1","title":"Extract diff rendering from mermaid_diff.rs into fm-render-term","description":"# Diff Rendering Extraction

Extract the complete diff rendering functionality from FrankenTUI's mermaid_diff.rs (1,830 lines) into frankenmermaid's fm-render-term crate.

## Reference Code
- Source: /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_diff.rs
- Lines: 1,830
- Purpose: Side-by-side diagram comparison with change highlighting

## Key Features to Extract

### 1. Diff Computation
- Node-level diff (added, removed, modified, unchanged)
- Edge-level diff (new connections, removed connections, changed labels)
- Cluster/subgraph diff
- Attribute diff (shapes, styles, classes)

### 2. Visual Representation
- Side-by-side layout
- Inline unified diff
- Change markers (color coding)
- Structural diff highlighting

### 3. Output Modes
- Terminal diff with ANSI colors
- Plain text diff (for CI/logging)
- Structured diff (JSON for tooling)

## Implementation Notes

The FrankenTUI diff system uses:
- LCS (Longest Common Subsequence) for sequence alignment
- Edit distance for similarity scoring
- Semantic grouping to minimize visual noise

This is NOT a blind copy - we must:
1. Remove ftui-specific dependencies
2. Use fm-core IR types directly
3. Integrate with fm-layout for positioning
4. Add comprehensive tests

## Acceptance Criteria

- All diff modes from reference implemented
- Unit tests for each diff algorithm
- Integration tests with real diagram pairs
- Performance within 2x of reference
- Deterministic output (same input pair => same diff)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:15:10.155700771Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:30.690031885Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diff","extraction","render"],"comments":[{"id":79,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:00Z"},{"id":96,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (per algorithm)
1. LCS algorithm correctness with known sequences
2. Edit distance calculation with boundary cases
3. Node diff detection (added, removed, modified, unchanged)
4. Edge diff detection with label changes
5. Cluster diff for nested subgraphs

### Integration Tests
1. Full diagram pair comparison with expected diff output
2. Diff output determinism (same pair = same diff)
3. Multiple output mode parity (terminal, plain, JSON)

### E2E Test Scripts
```bash
# scripts/test-diff-rendering.sh
fm-cli diff diagram1.mmd diagram2.mmd --format=json | jq '.changes'
fm-cli diff diagram1.mmd diagram2.mmd --format=terminal
```

### Logging Requirements
- RUST_LOG=fm_render_term::diff=debug for diff computation trace
- JSON structured logs for change detection steps
- Performance timing for LCS/edit-distance computation","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:24Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2nw.10","title":"Complete all remaining diagram type parsers (12 types)","description":"# Complete All Remaining Diagram Type Parsers

Implement parsers for all remaining Mermaid diagram types not yet supported to achieve 100% type coverage.

## Current Status

Implemented (12 types):
- flowchart ✓
- sequence ✓
- class ✓
- state ✓
- er ✓
- gitgraph ✓
- gantt ✓
- pie ✓
- mindmap ✓
- timeline ✓
- journey ✓
- quadrant ✓

## Remaining (12 types)

### Priority 1 (Common)
1. **sankey** - Flow diagrams with width-proportional edges
2. **c4context** - C4 architecture context diagrams
3. **c4container** - C4 container diagrams
4. **c4component** - C4 component diagrams

### Priority 2 (Moderate)
5. **c4dynamic** - C4 dynamic diagrams
6. **c4deployment** - C4 deployment diagrams
7. **architecture** - Architecture diagrams
8. **block** - Block diagrams

### Priority 3 (Specialized)
9. **kanban** - Kanban boards
10. **xychart** - XY charts
11. **radar** - Radar/spider charts
12. **treemap** - Treemap visualizations

## Implementation Pattern

For each diagram type:

```rust
fn parse_<type>(input: &str, builder: &mut IrBuilder) {
    // 1. Skip header line
    // 2. Parse type-specific directives
    // 3. Parse nodes/elements
    // 4. Parse relationships/edges
    // 5. Add warnings for unsupported syntax
}
```

## Per-Type Requirements

### Sankey
- Source → Target with value
- Auto-calculated widths
- Color groups

### C4 Types
- Person, System, Container, Component elements
- Relationship with description
- Boundary grouping
- External vs internal marking

### Architecture
- Groups and services
- Junctions and edges
- Icons and labels

### Block
- Columns and blocks
- Arrows between blocks
- Spacing directives

### Kanban
- Lanes (todo, doing, done)
- Cards with metadata
- WIP limits

### XY Chart
- Axes with ranges
- Line, bar, area series
- Data points

### Radar
- Axes (spokes)
- Data series
- Fill areas

### Treemap
- Hierarchical data
- Size values
- Color mapping

## Testing Requirements

Each type needs:
- 5+ positive test cases
- 3+ error recovery cases
- 1+ large input stress test
- Golden output comparison

## Acceptance Criteria

- All 24 diagram types parseable
- Detection working for all types
- Error recovery for malformed input
- Test coverage for each type
- Documentation of syntax supported","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:17:54.309853176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:31:13.861228645Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagram-types","parser"],"comments":[{"id":74,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:00Z"},{"id":103,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Per-Diagram-Type Tests
For each of 12 remaining types:
1. Basic syntax parsing
2. All statement types supported
3. Direction/orientation handling
4. Style/class application
5. Error recovery edge cases

### Diagram Types to Implement
- Quadrant chart, ZenUML, Timeline, Mindmap
- Sankey, User Journey, C4, Architecture
- Kanban, Block, Radar/XY, Treemap/Requirement/Packet

### Corpus Tests
Use mermaid-js test corpus for compatibility:
1. Parse all mermaid-js examples
2. Compare IR structure with expected
3. Track parser coverage metrics

### Regression Tests
Golden output for each diagram type sample

### Logging Requirements
- Parse trace with token/AST details
- Unsupported feature warnings with spans
- Confidence scores per parse result","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:12Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw.11","title":"Extract full canvas rendering from FrankenTUI canvas.rs","description":"# Full Canvas Rendering Extraction

Complete extraction of pixel-level canvas rendering from FrankenTUI's canvas.rs (1,651 lines) for fm-render-canvas.

## Reference Code
- Source: /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/canvas.rs
- Lines: 1,651
- Purpose: Pixel-perfect canvas rendering for web/WASM

## Current State

fm-render-canvas has basic rendering (~2,200 lines) but needs:
- Full shape rasterization
- Anti-aliased edge drawing
- Text rendering with font metrics
- Layer compositing
- Viewport management

## Key Features to Extract

### 1. Shape Rasterization
- Arc and circle drawing (Bresenham)
- Ellipse drawing
- Polygon filling
- Rounded rectangle corners
- All node shape variants

### 2. Edge Rendering
- Line drawing with thickness
- Anti-aliased lines
- Bezier curve approximation
- Arrow head rendering
- Dashed/dotted patterns

### 3. Text Rendering
- Glyph positioning
- Font metrics integration
- Text baseline alignment
- Word wrapping on canvas
- Emoji/Unicode support

### 4. Compositing
- Layer management
- Alpha blending
- Clipping regions
- Dirty rectangle tracking

### 5. Viewport System
- Pan and zoom
- Fit-to-content
- Scroll handling
- High-DPI support

### 6. Performance Features
- Dirty region optimization
- Offscreen buffer caching
- Progressive rendering
- Web Worker offloading

## Canvas API Integration

The reference uses:
- 2D canvas context methods
- Path2D for complex shapes
- ImageData for pixel access
- requestAnimationFrame for animation

fm-render-canvas needs:
- Canvas2dContext trait abstraction
- MockCanvas for testing
- web-sys integration (behind feature flag)

## WASM Considerations

- Memory management for large diagrams
- SharedArrayBuffer for workers
- WebGPU fallback path
- Mobile touch handling

## Acceptance Criteria

- All shapes from reference rendered
- Anti-aliased edges working
- Text rendering accurate
- Viewport interaction working
- Performance: 60fps for 200-node diagrams
- WASM demo rendering correctly","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:18:11.678931909Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:31:21.610990767Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["canvas","extraction","render","wasm"],"comments":[{"id":73,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.11","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:27:59Z"},{"id":104,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.11","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
1. Canvas primitive drawing (rect, circle, path, text)
2. Coordinate transformation correctness
3. Color handling (RGB, HSL, named colors)
4. Font metrics approximation accuracy

### Integration Tests
1. Full diagram render to mock canvas
2. Pixel-accurate comparison with golden bitmaps
3. HiDPI/scaling correctness
4. Clip region handling

### Cross-Platform Tests
1. Native canvas (if implemented)
2. WASM canvas via headless browser
3. OffscreenCanvas support

### Visual Regression
Generate PNG output and compare with golden images:
- Threshold: <1% pixel difference
- Per-diagram-type golden files

### Logging Requirements
- Draw call trace for debugging
- Render timing breakdown
- Canvas state stack trace","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw.12","title":"Implement adversarial input handling and security hardening","description":"# Adversarial Input Handling and Security Hardening (Parity and Shared Hardening)

Goal:
- Enforce robust security and bounded runtime behavior for untrusted inputs while preserving parity expectations.

Scope:
- Integrate shared security corpus and SVG sanitization policy.
- Validate parser, layout, and render behavior against adversarial and malformed inputs.

Verification and logging requirements:
- Unit tests for limits, sanitizer decisions, and threat classification.
- Integration tests for parser to layout to render hardening paths.
- E2E security sweeps with deterministic verdicts and reproducible artifacts.
- Structured logs must include threat class, trigger, sanitizer action, budget usage, fallback path, and pass fail reason.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:18:31.976024788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:32.266989287Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hardening","parser","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.12","depends_on_id":"bd-106","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:59.790885694Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.12","depends_on_id":"bd-1y5.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.427788918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.12","depends_on_id":"bd-2hq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.306699631Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.12","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:59.701233747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":105,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.12","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Adversarial Input Corpus
1. Extremely long node IDs (>10K chars)
2. Deeply nested structures (>100 levels)
3. Massive edge counts (>100K edges)
4. Malformed Unicode sequences
5. Control characters in labels
6. Escape sequence injection attempts

### Security Tests
1. No stack overflow on recursive input
2. Memory bounded for any input size
3. No DoS via pathological input
4. XSS prevention in SVG output
5. No command injection in CLI

### Fuzz Testing
Use cargo-fuzz with sanitizers:
```bash
cargo +nightly fuzz run fuzz_parser -- -max_len=100000
```

### Hardening Validation
1. All panics converted to Result errors
2. Resource limits enforced at all entry points
3. Graceful degradation under attack

### Logging Requirements
- Security-relevant events logged
- Rate of rejected inputs tracked
- Suspicious pattern detection","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw.13","title":"Complete DOT format parser with full Graphviz compatibility","description":"# Complete DOT Format Parser

Extend fm-parser's DOT parser to achieve high compatibility with Graphviz DOT format, enabling import of existing DOT diagrams.

## Reference Code
- Source: /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/dot_parser.rs
- Lines: 1,592
- Purpose: Parse Graphviz DOT format into shared IR

## Current State

fm-parser has basic DOT support (~500 lines) but needs:
- Full attribute parsing
- Subgraph/cluster support
- Edge properties
- Record shapes
- HTML labels

## Key Features to Implement

### 1. Graph Structure
- digraph (directed)
- graph (undirected)
- strict graphs (no multi-edges)
- subgraph and cluster

### 2. Node Attributes
- shape (box, circle, diamond, record, etc.)
- label (plain and HTML)
- style (solid, dashed, filled)
- color, fillcolor
- fontname, fontsize
- width, height
- pos (for importing positioned graphs)

### 3. Edge Attributes
- label
- style (solid, dashed, dotted)
- color
- arrowhead, arrowtail
- headlabel, taillabel
- constraint

### 4. Graph Attributes
- rankdir (TB, LR, BT, RL)
- bgcolor
- splines
- nodesep, ranksep
- compound

### 5. Special Features
- Record shapes (nested fields)
- HTML-like labels
- Edge ports (n, ne, e, se, s, sw, w, nw)
- ID quoting rules
- Comments (// and /* */)
- Preprocessor directives

### 6. Compatibility Levels

| Level | Features |
|-------|----------|
| Basic | digraph, nodes, edges |
| Standard | Attributes, clusters |
| Extended | Records, HTML labels |
| Full | Ports, preprocessor |

## Mapping to IR

```
DOT node → IrNode
DOT edge → IrEdge  
DOT subgraph → IrCluster
DOT attribute → IrNode.classes or style hints
```

## Testing

- Graphviz test suite compatibility
- Round-trip: DOT → IR → DOT
- Large real-world DOT files
- Edge cases from Graphviz docs

## Acceptance Criteria

- All common DOT features parsed
- Attribute mapping to IR complete
- Record shapes working
- HTML labels handled
- Test coverage: Graphviz examples
- Documentation of compatibility level","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:18:49.361263986Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:32.421290930Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dot","graphviz","parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.13","depends_on_id":"bd-106","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:59.879327938Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":91,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.13","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging.  {"id":"bd-2nw.14","title":"Implement advanced edge routing with orthogonal and spline paths","description":"# Advanced Edge Routing

Implement sophisticated edge routing algorithms from FrankenTUI to support orthogonal routing, curved edges, and proper edge-node avoidance.

## Reference Code
- Source: /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/diagram_layout.rs
- Lines: ~800 (routing section)
- Purpose: High-quality edge paths

## Current State

fm-layout has basic edge routing but lacks:
- Orthogonal (right-angle) routing
- Node avoidance
- Edge-edge avoidance
- Port assignment
- Curved edge smoothing

## Key Features to Implement

### 1. Orthogonal Router

**Algorithm**: Visibility graph + A* pathfinding

Steps:
1. Build obstacle grid (nodes as rectangles)
2. Compute visibility graph
3. Find shortest orthogonal path
4. Apply bend minimization
5. Assign edge channels

**Properties:**
- O(n²) visibility computation
- O(e log n) pathfinding per edge
- Guaranteed to find path if exists

### 2. Edge Channel Assignment

When multiple edges share a path segment:
1. Compute channel requirements
2. Assign channels to minimize crossings
3. Apply channel-specific offsets
4. Preserve edge ordering

### 3. Port Assignment

For nodes with multiple edges:
1. Group edges by direction
2. Assign port positions
3. Minimize edge crossings at ports
4. Handle multi-edge bundles

### 4. Curved Edge Smoothing

Convert orthogonal paths to smooth curves:
1. Identify corner points
2. Compute Bezier control points
3. Generate cubic Bezier segments
4. Preserve tangent continuity

### 5. Node Avoidance

Ensure edges don't cross through nodes:
1. Detect potential crossings
2. Route around obstacles
3. Minimize detour length
4. Handle nested clusters

### 6. Long Edge Handling

For edges spanning multiple layers:
1. Create dummy nodes at each layer
2. Route through dummies
3. Align dummy nodes for straightness
4. Handle wide nodes

## Rendering Integration

Edge paths output as:
```rust
pub enum EdgePath {
    Straight { from: Point, to: Point },
    Orthogonal { segments: Vec<(Point, Point)> },
    Bezier { segments: Vec<BezierCubic> },
}
```

## Performance Targets

| Graph Size | Routing Time |
|------------|--------------|
| 100 nodes | <50ms |
| 500 nodes | <200ms |
| 1000 nodes | <500ms |

## Acceptance Criteria

- Orthogonal routing working
- No edge-node intersections
- Port assignment minimizing crossings
- Curved edges option available
- Visual parity with FrankenTUI
- Performance within targets","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:07.733066831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:32.589949576Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["edges","layout","routing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.14","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:51.788304429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":72,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.14","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:27:59Z"},{"id":107,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.14","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Orthogonal Routing Tests
1. Channel assignment correctness
2. No edge crossings through nodes
3. Minimum bend count
4. Path length optimization

### Spline Path Tests
1. Bezier control point calculation
2. Smooth curve through waypoints
3. No self-intersecting curves
4. Consistent curvature

### Integration Tests
1. Full diagram with mixed edge styles
2. Layout determinism with edge routing
3. Performance with many edges (>1000)

### Visual Regression
Golden output for edge routing scenarios:
- Dense graphs with many crossings
- Sparse graphs with long paths
- Hierarchical layouts

### Logging Requirements
- Routing algorithm trace
- Channel occupancy map
- Bend points selected","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw.15","title":"Build automated feature parity verification against FrankenTUI","description":"# Automated Feature Parity Verification against FrankenTUI

Goal:
- Generate objective parity evidence that combines capability metadata, E2E telemetry, and proof artifacts into release consumable reports.

Scope:
- Build feature matrix generation from shared capability sources and runtime evidence.
- Compare reference and FM outputs semantically and perceptually with explicit confidence tags.
- Publish machine readable and human readable parity dashboards.

Verification and logging requirements:
- Unit tests for matrix generation, scoring rules, and threshold evaluation.
- Integration tests for ingestion of reference capture plus CLI and WASM artifacts.
- E2E parity runs with deterministic replay and triage outputs.
- Structured logs must include feature id, support state, reference verdict, FM verdict, delta type, confidence, artifact links, and pass fail reason.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:26.913410616Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:32.757022782Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:41.776755747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:42.120837335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:42.208363086Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:41.866478867Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:41.952552130Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:42.036757681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2xl.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.913294434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.550949443Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.24","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.793558913Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.674886281Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":108,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Feature Parity Matrix
Automated verification against FrankenTUI:

### Automated Comparison
```bash
# scripts/parity-check.sh
for sample in corpus/*.mmd; do
  frankentui_output=$(frankentui render $sample)
  frankenmermaid_output=$(fm-cli render $sample)
  compare_outputs $frankentui_output $frankenmermaid_output

```

- Prioritized gap list","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:43Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2nw.2","title":"Extract minimap rendering from mermaid_minimap.rs into fm-render-term","description":"# Minimap Rendering Extraction

Extract the complete minimap functionality from FrankenTUI's mermaid_minimap.rs (1,474 lines) into frankenmermaid's fm-render-term crate.

## Reference Code
- Source: /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_minimap.rs
- Lines: 1,474
- Purpose: Overview visualization of large diagrams with viewport indicator

## Key Features to Extract

### 1. Minimap Computation
- Proportional scaling to fit overview area
- Node density-based detail level selection
- Viewport rectangle calculation
- Pan/zoom coordinate mapping

### 2. Rendering Modes
- Terminal minimap (block characters)
- Simple minimap (ASCII only)
- Colored minimap (highlight regions)

### 3. Viewport Interaction
- Viewport position calculation
- Click-to-navigate coordinate mapping
- Viewport size indicator

### 4. Performance Features
- Lazy rendering (only visible regions)
- Cached minimap with dirty tracking
- Progressive detail rendering

## Implementation Notes

The FrankenTUI minimap uses:
- Downsampling algorithms for node representation
- Edge simplification (straight lines only)
- Color quantization for terminal output

Key adaptations needed:
1. Remove ftui-style dependencies
2. Integrate with fm-layout positioned nodes
3. Support multiple output targets (term, SVG, canvas)
4. Add streaming/progressive rendering

## Acceptance Criteria

- All minimap modes from reference implemented
- Unit tests for scaling/viewport math
- Integration with fm-render-term
- Visual regression tests
- Performance: <50ms for 1000-node diagrams","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:15:27.959621218Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:30.845005356Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extraction","minimap","render"],"comments":[{"id":78,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging.  {"id":"bd-2nw.3","title":"Complete Sugiyama coordinate assignment with Brandes-Köpf algorithm","description":"# Sugiyama Coordinate Assignment Completion

Implement the complete Brandes-Köpf coordinate assignment algorithm from FrankenTUI's mermaid_layout.rs to achieve pixel-perfect layout parity.

## Reference Code
- Source: /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_layout.rs
- Lines: ~3,000 (coordinate assignment section)
- Algorithm: Brandes-Köpf (2001) with enhancements

## Current State

fm-layout has basic coordinate assignment but lacks:
- Proper block alignment
- Median positioning
- Compact horizontal assignment
- Type 1/Type 2 conflict resolution

## Key Features to Implement

### 1. Vertical Alignment Phase
- Mark type-1 conflicts (where edges cross)
- Mark type-2 conflicts (long edges)
- Determine median alignment
- Handle multi-edge nodes

### 2. Horizontal Compaction Phase
- Block building (aligned node groups)
- Class separation computation
- Minimum width assignment
- Sink-based coordinate shifting

### 3. Four-Way Computation
- Upper-left alignment
- Upper-right alignment
- Lower-left alignment
- Lower-right alignment
- Final: median of four positions

### 4. Edge Straightening
- Long edge dummy alignment
- Port assignment
- Bend minimization

## Algorithm Properties (Alien-Artifact-Coding)

- Time complexity: O(n) where n = nodes + dummy nodes
- Space complexity: O(n)
- Determinism: Stable ordering guarantees identical output
- Proof: Each phase preserves layer assignment invariant

## Implementation Notes

FrankenTUI uses bidirectional sweep with:
1. LEFT_UP, RIGHT_UP, LEFT_DOWN, RIGHT_DOWN passes
2. Median of 4 positions for final coordinates
3. Compact representation via class graph

Critical: Preserve exact FrankenTUI behavior for determinism verification.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All 4-way alignment implemented
- Visual parity with FrankenTUI reference
- Property tests for invariants
- Performance: <10ms for 100-node graphs
- Determinism proof (golden tests)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:15:44.540525239Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:31.001816418Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithm","layout","sugiyama"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vb9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:51.693988459Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":77,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:00Z"},{"id":109,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Algorithm Correctness Tests
1. Vertical alignment phase preserves layer assignment
2. Block building creates valid aligned groups
3. Class separation computation is correct
4. Horizontal compaction minimizes width

### Four-Way Computation Tests
1. Upper-left alignment produces valid coordinates
2. Upper-right alignment is mirror of upper-left
3. Lower-left alignment respects reversed layers
4. Lower-right alignment combines both mirrors
5. Final coordinate is median of four results

### Property Tests (proptest)
1. All node coordinates are finite
2. No horizontal overlaps within layers
3. Layer gaps are consistent
4. Total width is minimal for given constraints

### Determinism Tests
1. Same graph = same coordinates (100 runs)
2. Cross-platform determinism (Linux/macOS/WASM)
3. Floating-point consistency

### Golden Tests
Reference outputs for:
- Simple DAG (5-10 nodes)
- Complex DAG with long edges
- DAG with clusters
- Wide flat graph

### Logging Requirements
- Per-phase coordinate dump
- Block structure trace
- Class assignment decisions","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:53Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw.4","title":"Implement source span tracking through full pipeline","description":"# Source Span Tracking Pipeline

Implement comprehensive source span tracking from input text through IR to rendered output, enabling click-to-source and precise error highlighting.

## Overview

Every element in the pipeline should carry its originating source span:
- Parser: Token spans → AST spans → IR spans
- Layout: IR spans → LayoutNode spans
- Render: LayoutNode spans → Output element spans

## Key Features

### 1. Parser Span Tracking
- Character-level offset tracking
- Line/column computation
- Multi-span nodes (e.g., chained edges)
- Comment and whitespace preservation

### 2. IR Span Preservation
- IrNode.span_primary: Main declaration span
- IrNode.span_all: All mentions in source
- IrEdge.span: Edge declaration span
- IrLabel.span: Label text span

### 3. Layout Span Propagation
- LayoutNode inherits IR spans
- Dummy nodes carry long-edge span
- Cluster spans from subgraph blocks

### 4. Render Span Emission
- SVG: data-source-span attributes
- Terminal: span → position mapping
- WASM: span query API

### 5. Source Map Generation
- V3 Source Map format for web tooling
- Bidirectional lookup (source→render, render→source)
- Incremental source map updates

## Implementation Notes

FrankenTUI tracks spans via:
- Span { start: usize, end: usize, line: u32, col: u32 }
- Multi-span aggregation for complex nodes
- Lazy line/column computation

We need:
1. Consistent span representation across crates
2. Span merging utilities
3. Source map serialization
4. Query APIs for tooling

## Use Cases

1. Error highlighting: Show exact location of parse errors
2. Click-to-source: Click rendered node → jump to source
3. Hover info: Show source context on hover
4. Diff correlation: Map diff regions to source changes

## Acceptance Criteria

- Spans preserved through parse → layout → render
- Source map emission for SVG output
- WASM span query API
- <1% performance overhead
- Integration with fm-cli validate output","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:16:03.139786220Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T15:34:41.954515952Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","parser","source-map"],"comments":[{"id":76,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:00Z"},{"id":95,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
1. Span Propagation Tests - verify span covers node/edge/subgraph declarations
2. Multi-span Tracking Tests - verify span_all collects all references
3. Layout Span Inheritance Tests - verify LayoutNode inherits IrNode spans

### Integration Tests
1. Full Pipeline Tests - Parse to Layout to Render with span assertions
2. Error Localization Tests - span points to exact error locations

### E2E Validation
1. Click-to-Source Tests - recover source span from rendered position
2. Unicode Tests - verify character-accurate boundaries

### Logging Requirements
- Structured JSON log entries with span fields
- Format: {stage, element_id, span: {start, end, line, col}}","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw.5","title":"Extract full terminal render capabilities from mermaid_render.rs","description":"# Terminal Render Full Extraction

Complete extraction of terminal rendering from FrankenTUI's mermaid_render.rs (9,352 lines) into fm-render-term.

## Reference Code
- Source: /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_render.rs
- Lines: 9,352
- Purpose: Full-featured terminal diagram rendering

## Current State

fm-render-term has basic terminal rendering (~2,000 lines) but lacks:
- Full shape library (all 20+ node shapes)
- Advanced edge rendering (curves, routing)
- Style application (colors, bold, dim)
- Unicode box drawing optimization
- Cluster rendering with borders

## Key Features to Extract

### 1. Shape Rendering (all shapes)
- Rectangle, rounded-rect, circle, ellipse
- Diamond, hexagon, parallelogram, trapezoid
- Stadium, subroutine, cylinder, database
- Double-circle, flag, note, document
- All asymmetric shapes

### 2. Edge Rendering
- Straight edges with arrow heads
- Orthogonal routed edges
- Curved edges (quadratic Bézier approximation)
- Edge labels (inline, above, below)
- Multi-segment long edges

### 3. Text Rendering
- Unicode-aware text wrapping
- Grapheme clustering for width calculation
- Truncation with ellipsis
- Vertical centering in shapes
- Label overflow handling

### 4. Style System
- ANSI color support (16, 256, true color)
- Bold, italic, underline, dim
- Background colors
- Style inheritance (class-based)
- Theme support

### 5. Cluster Rendering
- Cluster borders (box drawing)
- Cluster titles
- Nested cluster support
- Cluster padding and margins

### 6. Performance Features
- Dirty region tracking
- Incremental redraw
- Virtual scrolling for large diagrams

## Implementation Notes

FrankenTUI uses:
- ftui-render trait system for drawing
- Cell-based canvas with dirty tracking
- Pre-computed glyph widths

We need to:
1. Create standalone RenderSurface trait
2. Implement TerminalSurface with crossterm/termion
3. Add mock surface for testing
4. Preserve rendering fidelity exactly

## Acceptance Criteria

- All 20+ node shapes rendered correctly
- Edge routing matches reference
- Style application complete
- Visual regression tests

CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:00Z"},{"id":98,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (per shape)
Test each of 20+ node shapes:
- Rect, rounded-rect, circle, ellipse, diamond
- Hexagon, parallelogram, trapezoid, stadium
- Subroutine, cylinder, database, double-circle
- Flag, note, document, asymmetric shapes

### Edge Rendering Tests
1. Straight edge with all arrow types (-->, --o, --x, etc.)
2. Orthogonal routed edges with turns
3. Curved edges (Bezier approximation)
4. Edge labels (inline, above, below positions)
5. Multi-segment long edges spanning layers

### Integration Tests
1. Full diagram rendering with mock terminal surface
2. Determinism: same input = same terminal output
3. Unicode box drawing correctness
4. Cluster border rendering with nested content

### Visual Regression
Golden output files for each diagram type + shape combination

### Logging Requirements
- Trace-level logs for shape drawing decisions
- Per-shape render timing
- Character cell usage statistics","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:37Z"}]}
